text:
Proposal: Enable the Rate Limiter with 20s of queuing
We propose to enable the connection rate limiter with a conservative rate limit and 20 seconds
of queueing. By conservative rate limit, we mean a rate limit that aims to preserve availability
and accept slight overload of the database by selecting a high value to avoid connection
rejection by selecting a high value. The queue size will be the minimum of 20s * acceptance rate
or 20'000 to ensure that increasing the acceptance rate does not lead to excessively large
queues.
We propose to configure 20 seconds of queuing as it is slightly lower than the highest default
value seen in drivers (PyMongo defaults to 20 seconds while Java/C/C++/PHP/Ruby defaults to
10 seconds). We refill the token bucket on disconnection to avoid metastable failures: the only
downside of selecting a higher value is a slightly higher memory usage.
